message:
fix(node): fix push_bounded ordering bug, harden symlink/process/release safety across all modules

Fix a systematic bug in the push_bounded helper function that exists in
~190 modules across the codebase: the old code pushed the item first and
then drained excess entries, meaning the vec momentarily exceeded its
cap by one element. The fix checks capacity BEFORE pushing and drains
the oldest entry to make room, ensuring the cap invariant is never
violated. The same fix applies to push_unique_event in provenance.rs.

Harden main.rs filesystem traversal in collect_incident_bundle_paths and
collect_local_registry_artifacts_in to use entry.file_type() instead of
path.is_dir()/is_file() — the old code followed symlinks, which could
escape the intended directory tree or loop. Symlinks are now explicitly
skipped.

Refactor run_command_capture_stdout to spawn dedicated reader threads
for stdout and stderr pipes BEFORE entering the wait loop. The old code
read pipes only after the child exited, which could deadlock if the
child filled its pipe buffer and blocked waiting for the parent to
drain. Both reader threads are now properly joined on all exit paths
including timeout and wait errors.

Add release entry path inspection (inspect_release_entry_path) that
walks each path component checking for symlinks, with a new
ReleaseEntryState enum to distinguish missing/file/blocked entries.
Extend ReleaseVerificationContext with blocked_artifacts and
blocked_signatures maps for tracking entries rejected during
verification.

Also includes incident trace event validation hardening across security
and operations modules: whitespace detection, empty label/payload
checks, hex case enforcement, sequence monotonicity, and timestamp
ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
